label: Structured state-space models (S4 family)

node.label: Structured state-space models (S4 family)
node.definition: Structured state-space models such as S4 parameterize long-memory linear dynamical systems so sequence convolution or recurrence scales to very long contexts.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Structured state-space models (S4 family) is to connect State-space transitions, Fourier, wavelet & spectral transforms to downstream capabilities including Selective state-space models (Mamba family), Time-series forecasting, anomaly & fraud detection.
examples: substrate for Selective state-space models (Mamba family)
substrate for Time-series forecasting, anomaly & fraud detection
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include State-space transitions, Fourier, wavelet & spectral transforms.

dependencies.rationale: Structured state-space models (S4 family) is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component State-space transitions.
Structured state-space models (S4 family) is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Fourier, wavelet & spectral transforms.
Structured state-space models (S4 family) is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Gating & multiplicative control.
Structured state-space models (S4 family) consumes, emits, or organizes information using Sequences, events & time series.

sentence_units.text: Structured state-space models such as S4 parameterize long-memory linear dynamical systems so sequence convolution or recurrence scales to very long contexts.

The role of Structured state-space models (S4 family) is to connect State-space transitions, Fourier, wavelet & spectral transforms to downstream capabilities including Selective state-space models (Mamba family), Time-series forecasting, anomaly & fraud detection.
